rationale: In the introduction, the citing paper positions itself as similar in spirit to Bijmolt et al. (2005), i.e., a follow-up meta-analysis that updates an earlier classic. In the procedure section, it explicitly states that it follows Bijmolt et al. (2005) in using the hierarchical meta-analytic model, indicating direct methodological borrowing rather than a passing mention. Later, it cites the 2005 paper's -2.62 price elasticity estimate to benchmark advertising elasticity in its managerial implications. These roles make the citation function substantively meaningful.
relation_description: The citing study applies Bijmolt et al. (2005) as a methodological template for updating elasticity generalizations, transferring its meta-analytic modeling approach to the advertising elasticity context. It also uses the cited paper's price-elasticity estimate as an interpretive benchmark for managerial discussion.
